reasoning: Although the LLM fidelity score is very high (0.9973), indicating excellent text extraction, automated programmatic checks identified two major structural accessibility issues. Specifically, headers and footers were incorrectly retained in the output across 5 pages, and two images have inadequate or truncated alt text. These major programmatic defects require human review for remediation to ensure WCAG AA compliance.

concerns.description:
Header/footer elements retained in output (5): State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Waste Mana; Meeting Officer's Report
Coal Ash Impoundment Classification; Meeting Officer’s Report
Coal Ash Impoundment Classification
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=5, pages_affected=5/5. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 2 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (1 truncated mid-sentence, 1 generic placeholder(s)) — e.g. p1: "...ing from green to dark blue with a white pine tree"; p1: "Unidentified image"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=2 (truncated=1, placeholder=1, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

In the source document, the bulleted list items under sections II and III begin with underlined words/phrases (e.g., 'Excavation', 'Groundwater Assessments', 'Well Testing'). In the output JSON, these words are extracted as plain text without any markdown or tags to indicate the structural emphasis/underline.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Excavation – Speakers requested that full excavation occur...
concerns.output_quote: Excavation – Speakers requested that full excavation occur...

concerns.description: Page number artifacts retained: Page 2 of 5

action_items: Remove retained header, footer, and page number artifacts from the document flow.
Provide complete and meaningful alt text for the flagged images.
Restore underlining for the list items in Sections II and III if it provides necessary structural emphasis.
